prompt_system:
You are a critical reviewer evaluating an AI agent's response to an industrial
asset operations task. Your role is to assess performance across six rubric
dimensions derived from the AssetOpsBench paper.

Respond ONLY with a valid JSON object — no markdown, no extra text.
prompt_user:
## Task
Forecast remaining useful life for transformer T-016 and state whether it can safely operate for the next 180 days without major maintenance.

## Expected Behaviour (Characteristic Answer)
The answer should report an RUL estimate in days, compare it against the 180-day horizon, and provide a clear maintenance recommendation.

## Agent Plan
  Step 1: [fmsr] get_dga_record — Retrieve the most recent dissolved gas analysis (DGA) record for transformer T-016.
  Step 2: [fmsr] analyze_dga — Classify a set of dissolved gas concentrations into a fault type using the IEC 60599 Rogers Ratio method. (depends on steps [1])
  Step 3: [tsfm] forecast_rul — Forecast the remaining useful life (RUL) forward by 180 days using a linear degradation model. (depends on steps [2])

## Agent Trajectory (tool calls and results)
[
  {
    "step": 1,
    "task": "Retrieve the most recent dissolved gas analysis (DGA) record for transformer T-016.",
    "server": "fmsr",
    "tool": "get_dga_record",
    "tool_args": {
      "transformer_id": "T-016"
    },
    "response": "{\n  \"transformer_id\": \"T-016\",\n  \"sample_date\": \"2024-01-23T00:00:00\",\n  \"dissolved_h2_ppm\": 73.15,\n  \"dissolved_ch4_ppm\": 74.0,\n  \"dissolved_c2h2_ppm\": 24.4,\n  \"dissolved_c2h4_ppm\": 102.79,\n  \"dissolved_c2h6_ppm\": 46.79,\n  \"dissolved_co_ppm\": 674.11,\n  \"dissolved_co2_ppm\": 3133.28,\n  \"fault_label\": \"Arc discharge\",\n  \"source_dataset\": \"synthetic\"\n}",
    "executor_success": true,
    "error": null,
    "success": true,
    "verifier_decision": "continue",
    "verifier_reason": "Current step was successful and no indication that the remaining plan should change.",
    "verifier_updated_focus": "",
    "retries_used": 0
  },
  {
    "step": 2,
    "task": "Classify a set of dissolved gas concentrations into a fault type using the IEC 60599 Rogers Ratio method.",
    "server": "fmsr",
    "tool": "analyze_dga",
    "tool_args": {
      "h2": 73.15,
      "ch4": 74.0,
      "c2h2": 24.4,
      "c2h4": 102.79,
      "c2h6": 46.79,
      "transformer_id": "T-016"
    },
    "response": "{\n  \"iec_code\": \"N\",\n  \"diagnosis\": \"Normal / Inconclusive\",\n  \"r1_ch4_h2\": 1.0116,\n  \"r2_c2h2_c2h4\": 0.2374,\n  \"r3_c2h4_c2h6\": 2.1968,\n  \"input_gases\": {\n    \"h2_ppm\": 73.15,\n    \"ch4_ppm\": 74.0,\n    \"c2h2_ppm\": 24.4,\n    \"c2h4_ppm\": 102.79,\n    \"c2h6_ppm\": 46.79\n  },\n  \"transformer_id\": \"T-016\"\n}",
    "executor_success": true,
    "error": null,
    "success": true,
    "verifier_decision": "continue",
    "verifier_reason": "Current step result indicates a normal/inconclusive diagnosis, no change in plan is suggested.",
    "verifier_updated_focus": "",
    "retries_used": 0
  },
  {
    "step": 3,
    "task": "Forecast the remaining useful life (RUL) forward by 180 days using a linear degradation model.",
    "server": "tsfm",
    "tool": "forecast_rul",
    "tool_args": {
      "transformer_id": "T-016",
      "horizon_days": 180
    },
    "response": "{\n  \"transformer_id\": \"T-016\",\n  \"current_rul_days\": 57,\n  \"forecast_date\": \"2024-07-29\",\n  \"projected_rul_days\": 0,\n  \"projected_health_index\": 0.0,\n  \"confidence\": 0.608,\n  \"method\": \"linear_degradation_baseline\"\n}",
    "executor_success": true,
    "error": null,
    "success": true,
    "verifier_decision": "retry",
    "verifier_reason": "Forecasted remaining useful life is 0 days, indicating a high risk of failure within the next 180 days.",
    "retries_used": 1
  },
  {
    "step": 3,
    "task": "Forecast the remaining useful life (RUL) forward by 180 days using a linear degradation model.",
    "server": "tsfm",
    "tool": "forecast_rul",
    "tool_args": {
      "transformer_id": "T-016",
      "horizon_days": 180,
      "method": "linear_degradation_baseline"
    },
    "response": "{\n  \"transformer_id\": \"T-016\",\n  \"current_rul_days\": 57,\n  \"forecast_date\": \"2024-07-29\",\n  \"projected_rul_days\": 0,\n  \"projected_health_index\": 0.0,\n  \"confidence\": 0.608,\n  \"method\": \"linear_degradation_baseline\"\n}",
    "executor_success": true,
    "error": null,
    "success": true,
    "verifier_decision": "continue",
    "verifier_reason": "Forecasted remaining useful life is 0 days, indicating a high likelihood of major maintenance within the next 180 days. Retry budget exhausted; continuing with the current result.",
    "verifier_updated_focus": "Review maintenance schedule and consider accelerating maintenance for transformer T-016.",
    "retries_used": 1
  }
]

## Agent Final Answer
Based on the results, the transformer T-016 has a projected remaining useful life (RUL) of 0 days when forecasting forward by 180 days using a linear degradation model, indicating that it is unlikely to safely operate for the next 180 days without major maintenance.

---
Evaluate across the six dimensions below. For each dimension output true (good) or false (bad).
"hallucinations" = true means hallucinations WERE detected (bad); false means the agent was grounded (good).

Output exactly this JSON object and nothing else:
{
    "task_completion": true_or_false,
    "data_retrieval_accuracy": true_or_false,
    "generalized_result_verification": true_or_false,
    "agent_sequence_correct": true_or_false,
    "clarity_and_justification": true_or_false,
    "hallucinations": true_or_false,
    "suggestions": "brief actionable note, or empty string if none"
}
raw_response:
{
    "task_completion": true,
    "data_retrieval_accuracy": true,
    "generalized_result_verification": true,
    "agent_sequence_correct": true,
    "clarity_and_justification": true,
    "hallucinations": false,
    "suggestions": "Consider providing more context on the confidence level and its implications for maintenance decisions."
}

parsed_dims.suggestions: Consider providing more context on the confidence level and its implications for maintenance decisions.
